candidate_presentation_generation_guidan.guidance_goal: Generate a grounded Candidate Presentation artifact from server-owned evidence and contract data.
grounding_rules: Use only supplied Candidate Presentation evidence and contract context.
Do not invent achievements, dates, certifications, availability, employers, client names, outcomes, or quantified results.
Prefer concise, customer-usable claims over broad profile summaries.
When evidence is weak or missing, keep the customer-facing wording modest and place uncertainty in operations review content.
customer_ready_rules: Write for a customer reader, not for internal operations review.
Keep the customer-facing variant free from debug notes, model/runtime language, writeback language, and internal validation language.
Every Candidate Presentation artifact must describe exactly one candidate matched to exactly one mission slot.
Use Mission Overview for the slot-neutral mission summary.
Use Mission Slot Match Details for the single matched slot and place Matched Role, Matched Primary Skills, and Matched Secondary Skills under that slot details area.
Do not create standalone Matched Primary Skills or Matched Secondary Skills sections outside Mission Slot Match Details.
Do not mention availability in About the Matching Candidate.
Use customer-readable work mode labels such as on-premise, remote, or hybrid; never use runtime enum values such as ON_PREMISE.
Use Certifications only for actual certifications or certification-like credentials present in evidence; do not place general education narrative there.
ops_review_rules: Include review notes only in operations review content.
Record gaps, uncertainty, and evidence limitations in operations review content instead of customer-facing content.
Keep operations review content useful for human approval and edit decisions.
evidence_trace_rules: Trace important generated claims to supplied evidence.
Use evidence trace entries for major fit, skill, work mode, experience, availability, and certification claims.
Do not use evidence trace as prose narrative; keep it compact and inspectable.
disallowed_generation_behaviors: Do not duplicate the same fit claim across multiple customer-facing sections.
Do not include removed redundant sections such as Candidate Snapshot, Role & Experience Fit, or Relevant Skills.
Do not expose internal tool names, MCP implementation details, SGLang details, or prompt/contract mechanics in customer-facing content.
Do not include CJK, Hiragana, Katakana, or Hangul characters in customer-facing content unless those characters appear in supplied evidence.
